message:
fix(ext-json): reject BLOBs in JSON functions; fix json_extract 1-arg edge case

json_quote: Change return type from String to Result<String> and return
an error for BLOB inputs instead of silently converting them to hex
X'...' literals. SQLite's json_quote() also errors on BLOBs — our
previous behavior was non-conformant and could produce invalid JSON
when downstream consumers don't expect SQL hex literals.

sqlite_to_json (used by json_array, json_object, etc.): Same fix —
return an error for BLOB inputs instead of hex-encoding them.

json_extract / JsonExtractFunc: When called with exactly 1 argument
(json, no paths), return NULL instead of erroring. This matches
SQLite's behavior where json_extract('{"a":1}') returns NULL. The
arity check now only errors on 0 arguments.

All affected tests updated to expect the new error/Result semantics.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
